description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head with a curved trunk and large ears, adorned with an ornate headdress and jewelry. The figure is seated cross-legged, with two arms raised and holding symbolic objects, while the other two hands rest on the knees. The surface is textured with intricate carvings, especially on the headdress, jewelry, and facial features, giving it a detailed and traditional appearance. The material appears to be a stone or ceramic with a weathered, off-white finish and subtle grayish shading in the recesses. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with visible garment folds and a strap crossing the back. This object is compact and solid, likely intended for display in a public or private urban space as a decorative or religious artifact.
description: A detailed, off-white sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, seated with ornate carvings and symbolic hand gestures. The figure features an elephant head, elaborate headdress, and traditional jewelry.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing Ganesha's face, trunk, headdress, jewelry, and all four arms with detailed carvings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the headdress, the profile of the trunk, and the raised left arm.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the head, garment folds, and a strap crossing the back, with minimal detail.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, trunk, and the right arm's gesture, as well as the layered headdress.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
